title: Shoulder Abduction and Adduction
description: Strengthens shoulder muscles and improves range of motion by lifting the arm sideways away from the body.
instructions:
1. Stand or sit tall with arms at your sides.
2. Slowly lift your arm sideways up to shoulder height.
3. Hold for 2-3 seconds.
4. Slowly lower back down.
5. Repeat 10-15 times per arm.

title: Shoulder Flexion
description: Improves shoulder mobility and strength by lifting the arm forward overhead.
instructions:
1. Stand or sit tall with arms at sides.
2. Slowly lift one arm forward and overhead.
3. Hold for 2-3 seconds.
4. Slowly lower back down.
5. Repeat 10-15 times per arm.

title: Squats
description: Strengthens quadriceps, glutes, and hamstrings while improving knee and hip mobility.
instructions:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart.
2. Slowly bend knees, pushing hips back as if sitting in a chair.
3. Keep chest upright and knees over toes.
4. Hold 1-2 seconds at the bottom.
5. Return to standing.
6. Repeat 10-15 times.

title: Lunges
description: Strengthens quadriceps, hamstrings, glutes, and improves balance and hip mobility.
instructions:
1. Stand upright with feet hip-width apart.
2. Step one leg forward and bend both knees to 90 degrees.
3. Keep front knee over ankle and back knee hovering above floor.
4. Hold 1-2 seconds.
5. Push back to standing.
6. Repeat 10 times per leg.

title: Wall Push-Up
description: A beginner-friendly upper body exercise that strengthens the chest, shoulders, and arms while reducing strain compared to floor push-ups.
instructions:
1. Stand facing a wall at arm’s length.
2. Place palms flat on the wall at shoulder height.
3. Bend elbows and slowly lean your body toward the wall.
4. Push through your palms to return to the starting position.
5. Repeat 10–15 times.

title: Straight Leg Raise
description: A strengthening exercise that targets the quadriceps and improves knee stability, commonly used in knee rehabilitation.
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with one leg bent and the other straight.
2. Tighten the thigh muscles of the straight leg.
3. Slowly lift the straight leg about 12–18 inches off the floor.
4. Hold for 3–5 seconds.
5. Slowly lower the leg back down.
6. Repeat 10–15 times per leg.

title: Glute Bridge
description: Strengthens the gluteal muscles and core while improving hip stability and lower back support.
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Keep arms at your sides.
3. Tighten your glutes and lift hips toward the ceiling.
4. Hold for 3–5 seconds at the top.
5. Slowly lower hips back to the floor.
6. Repeat 10–15 times.

title: Bird Dog Exercise
description: A core stability exercise that strengthens the back, hips, and abdominal muscles while improving balance and coordination.
instructions:
1. Start on hands and knees with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips.
2. Extend your right arm forward and left leg backward.
3. Keep your back flat and core engaged.
4. Hold for 3–5 seconds.
5. Return to starting position and switch sides.
6. Repeat 10 times per side.

title: Dead Bugs
description: A core stability exercise that strengthens the abdominal muscles while protecting the lower back.
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with arms extended toward the ceiling and knees bent at 90 degrees.
2. Slowly lower your right arm and left leg toward the floor while keeping your lower back pressed to the mat.
3. Return to the starting position.
4. Repeat with the opposite arm and leg.
5. Perform 10–15 repetitions per side.

title: Flutter Kicks
description: A lower abdominal and hip flexor exercise that improves core strength and endurance.
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with hands under your glutes for support.
2. Extend both legs straight off the floor.
3. Alternately lift and lower your legs in a fluttering motion, keeping them straight.
4. Perform for 20–30 seconds per set.

title: Dips
description: An upper body exercise that strengthens triceps, shoulders, and chest using bodyweight.
instructions:
1. Sit on the edge of a sturdy chair or bench.
2. Place hands on the edge, fingers facing forward.
3. Slide your hips off the chair and bend elbows to lower your body.
4. Stop when elbows reach ~90 degrees.
5. Push back up to starting position.
6. Repeat 10–12 times.

title: Crunches
description: A classic abdominal exercise that strengthens the rectus abdominis and improves core stability.
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Place hands behind your head without pulling neck.
3. Lift your shoulders off the floor while engaging the core.
4. Hold 1–2 seconds at the top.
5. Slowly lower back down.
6. Repeat 10–15 times.
tips: focus on abdominal contraction, not momentum

title: Hip Thrust
description: Strengthens glutes, hamstrings, and core while improving hip stability and lower back support.
instructions:
1. Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench or sturdy surface and knees bent, feet flat on the floor.
2. Press through your heels and lift your hips toward the ceiling.
3. Hold at the top for 2–3 seconds.
4. Slowly lower hips back down.
5. Repeat 10–15 times.
precautions: keep feet flat on the floor
contraindications: recent hip or lower back surgery

title: Step-Ups
description: A functional lower-body exercise that strengthens quadriceps, glutes, and improves balance and coordination.
instructions:
1. Stand in front of a sturdy step or bench.
2. Step up with one foot, pressing through the heel.
3. Bring the other foot up to stand fully on the step.
4. Step back down one foot at a time.
5. Repeat 10–12 times per leg.
tips: press through heel of stepping foot
